sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, event_date DATE, description VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, description) VALUES (1, DATE '2020-01-01', 'New Year'), (2, DATE '2020-02-29', 'Leap Day'), (3, DATE '2020-03-22', 'Spring Equinox');
SELECT id, description FROM test_dates WHERE dayofweek(event_date) > 5;
SELECT id, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE dayofweek(event_date) > (SELECT dayofweek(DATE '2020-01-01'));
CREATE TABLE event_details (event_id INT, detail VARCHAR(100), FOREIGN KEY (event_id) REFERENCES test_dates(id));
INSERT INTO event_details (event_id, detail) VALUES (1, 'Celebration'), (2, 'Unique occurrence'), (1, 'Party');

SELECT td.id, td.description, COUNT(ed.detail) AS detail_count FROM test_dates td LEFT JOIN event_details ed ON td.id = ed.event_id GROUP BY td.id HAVING dayofweek(td.event_date) > 5 AND COUNT(ed.detail) > 0;
SELECT MAX(event_date) AS max_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date < (SELECT MIN(event_date) FROM test_dates WHERE id > 1);

SELECT event_date, dayofweek(event_date) FROM test_dates WHERE dayofweek(event_date) = (SELECT dayofweek(event_date) FROM test_dates WHERE id = 3) OR event_date > (SELECT MAX(event_date) FROM test_dates WHERE dayofweek(event_date) > 6);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_numbers WHERE num > (SELECT MAX(num) FROM test_numbers WHERE num IS NOT NULL);
SELECT dayofweek(CAST(num AS DATE)) FROM test_numbers WHERE num IS NOT NULL;
SELECT event_date FROM test_dates WHERE dayofweek(event_date) >= (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT event_id) FROM event_details WHERE detail IS NULL);
